contract_scope: Client-to-server envelope for inference generation. Consumed by POST /api/infer (#M-10, non-streaming HTTP) and the WS 'infer.start' event (#M-11, IIBIN binary token stream). The envelope is pinned here so both transports share one validator (domain/inference/inference_request.php) and the same canonical rejection codes from api-ws-contract.catalog.json.
honesty_rules: session_id is client-supplied; the backend does NOT trust it for authentication. It is a client-side trace identifier that the server echoes back in token-frame headers and transcript persistence (#M-16). A future leaf adds server-managed session binding.

model_selector.prefer_local is a hint, not a guarantee. If a locally registered model does not fit the node profile at selection time (#M-6), the selector may return a different winner or reject the request with 'model_fit_unavailable'.

sampling.seed is optional; when present the backend MUST plumb it to llama.cpp as '-s <seed>' so two calls with the same seed + same prompt + same model produce the same completion. Omitting it yields a fresh random seed per call — never a silent default to 0.
sampling.max_tokens is hard-capped by the backend at 8192 to refuse unbounded generation. A lower cap may apply if the loaded model's context_length is smaller (enforced at #M-10).

prompt is a single string. The backend always submits it to llama.cpp's /v1/chat/completions, which applies the chat template baked into the GGUF (ChatML / Llama-2 chat / whatever-the-model-expects). Clients can now supply a multi-turn `messages` array (#T-1) for chat memory; when present, `prompt` is optional and — if supplied — is appended as the final user turn, letting the client pass prior history without mutating it. When `messages` is absent the envelope behaves exactly as before (single {system?, user:prompt} turn).

stream is authoritative: 'false' forces POST /api/infer to return one full JSON envelope (#M-10); 'true' is only valid on WS 'infer.start' (#M-11). The HTTP route rejects stream=true with 'invalid_request_envelope' so callers can't silently request a streaming response they cannot consume.

prompt.note: required when messages[] is absent; optional when messages[] is present (appended as a trailing user turn if supplied)

messages.note: multi-turn chat history (#T-1). When present the server passes it verbatim to /v1/chat/completions, optionally prefixed by system and suffixed by prompt. Replaces prompt as the minimum user-signal requirement.

seed.note: missing means fresh random seed per call

frequency_penalty.note: OpenAI-compatible; scales penalty with token frequency in recent output. #T-3: combats small-model echo-collapse where the assistant copies the previous reply.

presence_penalty.note: OpenAI-compatible; penalizes any token that has appeared in the output at all. #T-3: pairs with frequency_penalty to break repetition loops.

rejection_codes.invalid_request_envelope: any required field missing; any field out-of-range; stream=true on the HTTP route; unknown top-level keys; wrong type for a required field
rejection_codes.model_not_found: model_selector did not match any registry row (model_name + quantization)
rejection_codes.model_fit_unavailable: registry row exists but no node in scope can host it (RAM/VRAM/quantization filter per #M-6)

details.field: dotted path of the failing leaf, e.g. sampling.temperature
details.reason: human-readable cause with the clamping rule
details.observed: the value the client sent (primitive types only; arrays/objects summarised)
